title: Level 799
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 799: "Pearls"}}{{Background|file=Have you seen the glowing letters?.png}}{{Collapse|num=1|initial=

[[File:BIF Logo.png|link=]] {{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''Director:''' Thomas Blue, Agent Iota. So glad you could make it. '''Blue:''' What have you got for me today, sir? I thought my unit was set to assist the [[Party Crashers]] in the partygoers' recent breach of [[The Hub|the Hub]]? '''Director:''' You are correct. The plans for K4 as a whole have not changed, except that... we have a more sinister, if subtler, problem on our hands. '''Blue:''' And what may that be? '''Director:''' It concerns... Agent Theta. '''Blue:''' Johnny Gravewalker? I haven't seen him in— oh. Is this about the recent passing of his daughter? He— he hasn't gone suicidal, has he? '''Director:''' Certainly not. But he has, in fact, gone rogue. '''Blue:''' Rogue, you say? '''Director:''' Days ago, Agent Theta was spotted by one of our agents loitering near [[the Smile Room]]. They— they didn't make it, but fortunately all the evidence was caught on camera. '''Blue:''' What do you mean? '''Director:''' Agent Theta has become driven insane by [[The Smile Room|the Smile Curse]]. He's but a husk of his former self, a cannibalistic killing machine driven by whatever twisted motivation lies behind that unhinged smile of his. '''Blue:''' That... That's horrible. But won't the [[The Smile Room|curse]] kill him eventually? What do I have to do with this? '''Director:''' Based on his appearance in the footage, we've been able to determine that he's been cursed for at least several days now; his body shows no signs of being near to death — other than the huge Glasgow smile plastered on his face, of course. Agent Iota, you must listen to me. Agent Theta was the most talented assassin the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] has ever had...
''...and thanks to muscle memory, he still is.'' '''Blue:''' I— I don't get it. Why are you telling me all this? '''Director:''' We have selected you, Agent Iota, to ''hunt him down'' and ''take him out.''
|final=|body={{Collapse |num=2 |initial=

You've been invited!
From: Johnny
To: {{USERNAME|You!}}

Dear {{USERNAME|wanderer}}, I am writing this to inform you that you have been invited to Johnny's Special Dinner Eventeeeeeeee! This will be happening on lnl/lol/lwl, at the time right:nowll, at my houseeeeeeeee

Save the date!
Once you arrive, we will have snacks.
|final= |body= {{Blurbg|file=Have you seen the glowing letters?.png}} {{Class Custom Image|class=Deadzone|hexagon=🕱|bg-image=Void.png|text-color=white|safety=Lethal Conditions|security=Constantly Fluctuating|entity=Presence of Single Friend Entity}} '''Level 799''' is the 800th level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=Have you seen the glowing letters?.png|caption=A room with no view.|hovertext=Find me.}} == Overview == '''Level 799''', otherwise known as "'''Pearls'''", is described as a modern household shrouded in darkness, consisting of several hallways and an upstairs and attic floor, along with a few light sources such as birthday candles illuminating the level. Scattered around the house are a wide range of other party items, such as hats or board games, noted to be arranged in a way and with written text prompting the wanderer to interact with and use them. The level will have entirely different themes depending on the wanderer's preferred type of birthday, but will always have the same persistent eerie mood. There is constant "music" playing, if one can call it that, which sounds to be a haphazard arrangement of musical notes played by random instruments. The music is quite fascinating due to its oddity to many, but it is best to not get distracted by it, as it may be one of the many ploys set up for wanderers. There is an apparent source of the "music", but as will be made clear later, it is best to not touch the turntable, or anything belonging to this level as a matter of fact. The level is subject to both Phenomena [[Phenomenon 14|14]] and [[Phenomenon 15|15]], meaning that in any direction the wanderer follows, they will be notice that they aren't going anywhere, and are merely going in a loop. Combined with the darkness initially masking this effect, this will leave them vulnerable. Alongside this, the wanderer will be completely isolated from all other instances of '''Level 799''' which are formed specifically for each person who enters, giving the wanderer a heavy feeling of unease due to the personalisation using each wanderer's own experiences with birthdays, with some even stating that they have witnessed their name appear in certain areas of the level spelled out with balloons, or on cards and cakes, which again, are poor attempts to put you at ease. There are three floors in total in which one can find the key fragments required for one to escape the level (see '''''Exits'''''). One should only attempt to use any source of light for a short time in order to find these fragments. The first floor is where one initially starts from, with it resembling a simple living room surrounded by hallways leading to other rooms, such as bathrooms and storage rooms. There is one particular section near the living room, the dining room, which is highly unrecommended to explore as it is quite a graphic mess, since it appears to be strewn with "leftovers", to put it lightly. Interacting with anything on this floor, whether it be the turntable or a television constantly displaying static, is strictly advised against, as this is where '''Johnny''' (see '''''Entities''''') is typically found to reside in. If one does manage to alert '''Johnny''', God forbid, there is one policy that may just save one's life if followed properly.
=== The R.A.O.H. Policy — Run Away Or Hide === ---- * If you are unsure of whether '''Johnny''' knows you're there or not, listen carefully to the pattern of his footsteps and decide whether to '''Run Away Or Hide'''. If they sound like they're going at a regular pace, this means that he is unsure if anybody is there, in which you can simply run away from your current spot. Running away from where you were standing will confuse '''Johnny''' temporarily, and if you're lucky enough, he might turn around and walk away, clearing the coast. If the footsteps sound like he's stumbling, that means '''Johnny''' is fully aware of your presence, in which you must hide for as long as possible until he looks elsewhere. Ideally, you should hide under a table, in a cupboard, or behind a couch.
For the second floor, which is accessible through a flight of stairs in the living room, you will find that it is an upstairs hallway containing two bedrooms and a bathroom. The first bedroom is extremely messy, and is filled with the black smoky substance that '''Johnny''' is comprised of, along with writing on the walls indicating that he had let out his burning rage in this room. The second bedroom appears to belong to '''Johnny''''s since deceased daughter, with the room being filled with her favourite things. There is nothing particularly notable about the third floor, which is an attic, besides the fact that it contains many cardboard boxes filled with several photos and items supposedly belonging to '''Johnny''''s family members. == Entities ==
{{{image|[[File:CUIcon.png|right|120px]]}}}Person of Interest: Johnathan Gravewalker '''Status:''' ''[[The Smile Room|Subridens]]''
'''Possible Location(s):''' Level 799
'''Known Affiliation(s):''' [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|Kilo-4]] ''(formerly)''
'''Last Seen:''' Level 799
{{Blurimage|file=Johnny is here.jpg|caption=Something lurks in the shadows.|hovertext=I'm always there.}} === Overview === ---- '''Johnathan Gravewalker''', also known as '''Agent Theta''' or '''Agent Θ''' and sometimes colloquially referred to as '''Johnny''', is the sole entity of '''Level 799'''. Not much is known about him, for he keeps to the shadows of the level and only comes into the light to strike; but what little information we have gained based on found footage reveal that he appears as a humanoid form, partially composed of an amorphous black substance comparable to the [[Entity 3|''Nigrum ingenem'']], and always with two shining eyes staring out of the darkness like milky-white pearls and an uncanny smile plastered on his murk-enshrouded face. === Behaviour === ---- '''Johnny''' is extremely hostile and will attack anyone at first sight, yet he appears to have impaired eyesight; albeit — unlike other beings with visual disabilities — none of his other senses seem to be amplified in any form. Thus, if one stays away from light and tries not to make any noise, it is quite unlikely that the entity will be able to find them; it is for this reason that one should, if one values their survival, never attempt to switch on the level's electric lights. In addition, '''Johnny''' has been known to attempt to lure wanderers out of their hiding place by speaking to them, trying to convince them that death at his hands is the only way; in certain cases, this stratagem has somehow managed to work in favour of the entity. Those who have been seen, heard or smelt out by '''Johnny''' face a bleak fate. The entity can move at incredible speeds, lunging forward to grasp his prey in his smoky, semi-tangible hands before proceeding to eat them alive. No other fate has ever been recorded for a victim of '''Johnny''', for it seems he has a constant, insatiable hunger for human flesh that drives him to these uncouth and gruesome actions. === History === ---- Many years ago, '''Johnny''' was a member of the elite [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] SWAT team known as K4, taking the alias of '''Agent Theta''' (stylised '''Agent Θ'''). Famous throughout the foundation, many of his colleagues knew him as the best assassin the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] has ever had; and because of this, he was assigned many solo missions by the group's Director, forbye his collaborative assignments alongside the rest of K4. Over his time as an agent of the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]], '''Johnny''' has successfully assassinated the corrupt leaders of many groups, a list which cannot be shown in this article due to confidentiality issues. However, '''Johnny''''s future as a K4 agent was made uncertain following the death of his beloved daughter, '''Pearl Gravewalker''', under mysterious circumstances. Eventually, two [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] SWAT teams found her body outside an instance of [[the Smile Room]], with a Glasgow smile carved into her deceased face. '''Johnny''' was excused from his work for several days to mourn his daughter and attend her funeral, yet the other agents of K4 still insisted on continuing their operations while the funeral took place — even '''Agent Iota''', or '''Thomas Blue''', who '''Johnny''' had once considered his best friend. Based on recorded footage and eyewitness accounts of '''Johnny''', he had found '''Agent Iota''''s refusal to attend his daughter's funeral an act of terrible betrayal, and decided that he could not even trust his closest friend. Subsequently, he seemingly went mad with grief and attempted to "rejoin" his daughter, seeking out [[the Smile Room]] and purposefully inflicting himself with [[The Smile Room|the Smile Curse]]. However, it seemed that the curse refused to function properly on his body, warping him into a semi-monstrous, anthropoid creature with a hungering for human flesh. Afterwards, '''Johnny''' presumably found '''Level 799''' and took up residence within, although several theories suggest that the level could actually be a [[Phenomenon 9|tulpa]] formed by '''Johnny''''s envisioning of the dream house in which he would live with his daughter, if they were not in the Backrooms and she had not met an untimely demise. Regardless of how he came to lurk in the level, '''Johnny''' appeared then to become its permanent resident, catching and eating wanderers who unknowingly roamed into it to sate his unending hunger — at least, that seems to be his last remaining purpose. The entity himself, though, proposes a different reason for his actions: he is only searching for a loyal acquaintance with whom to spend the remainder of his wretched days. Because, after all...
''He would do anything for a friend.''

[[File:goddamn.jpg|center|500px|link=]]
== Colonies and Outposts == Due to its hostile conditions, no colonies or outposts have been established in '''Level 799'''. == Entrances and Exits == === How Did You End Up Here? === Finding an invitation on any level (mainly on party-themed or nostalgic levels) and reading it will seamlessly transition one's surroundings into this level, which is why most are often unaware of when they've entered here. This still works if multiple people are holding the invitation at once. Showing up uninvited by noclipping one's way in through the same levels containing these invitations will "toss" one into another wanderer's version of the level and draw '''Johnny''''s attention to oneself, directly violating the '''R.A.O.H. Policy''' and putting both wanderers concerned at risk. === The Only Way Out === If one wishes to exit this level, which they most likely will, they will have to hassle with '''Johnny''' in order to find silver magnetic key fragments scattered around the house, which appear to be pieces of an ordinary fidget toy that notably once belonged to '''Johnny''''s daughter. Finding all seven of the key's fragments will cause '''Johnny''' to instantly become aware of one's location regardless of where one currently is, beginning to pursue one until they have managed to unlock the correct door and exit into any [[Special:RandomInCategory/Class 1|Class 1]] level. This may be tricky, but keep in mind that only one of the doors has a lock, so it is recommended to mark its location beforehand. == Addendum ==

[[File:BIF Logo.png|link=]] {{Collapse|num=3|initial=> File outdated. View new file? <|final=> Close file? <|body=
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''Thomas Blue''' had been dead for years. Legend had it he was murdered in cold blood on a mission to take out his former friend, '''Johnathan Gravewalker'''. But in the years that followed, a new '''Agent Iota''' was assigned: '''Thomas''''s son, '''Samford Blue'''. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''Sam''' walked through the dark halls of '''Level 799''', glancing around warily. He caught a glimpse of something off: the light reflecting in the wrong direction in a thin line across the floor; and on closer inspection, he found a ridge. Carefully, he stooped and prised the trapdoor open, peering into the cavity beyond, where a cluster of small round landmines sat waiting — each and every one branded with the eagle-headed logo of the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] "You're a smart one, aren't you?" he muttered, letting the trapdoor fall closed. And then in a loud call: "Come out, wherever you are, '''Johnny'''! I know you're watching me." A shadowy figure stepped forth from the murk, his eyes glinting dangerously, his mouth curled into a massive smile. "'''Tom''''s kid, are you?" said '''Johnny''', eyeing '''Sam''' with his milky, purblind orbs. "Your father was the worst friend anyone could ask for. The rumours are true, my pearl; I killed him all those years ago, killed him for playing me like some disposable playing-card. I'll remind you: I saw him as a friend, but his negligence for all that I held dear made me realise just how much of a duplicitous little sneak he was." "My father was a great person," said '''Sam''', his voice unwavering, "You claim you were his friend, but clearly you know nothing about him." '''Johnny''' let out a bark of laughter. "Then, my pearl, perhaps he and I were never friends. Nothing you say is going to make an ''iota'' of difference regarding my feelings about him." His smile seemed to widen, if that was even possible. "My daughter is dead. Dead. How would you feel if your best friends had not come to your father's funeral? The pain of his ignorance made it so much harder. That's why I killed him. But my business here is far from done. As far as I'm concerned, '''Tom''' always loved you. You were his ''pearl''." '''Sam''' forced himself to look the shadowy figure in the eye. "So?" he asked, "What does it matter if my father loved me?" "My pearl died," said '''Johnny''' quietly. "And so will his." But '''Sam''' had other ideas. He raised his handheld transceiver to his mouth and commanded, "Now." {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Every booby trap '''Johnny''' had set with [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] equipment detonated. The house exploded in a blinding wave of heat, wreathing the level in bright orange flame, and leaving but a mass of debris and rubble floating in an empty [[The Void|Void]]. Yet '''Johnny''' emerged, unscathed. "Oh, my pearl," he chuckled, as if he were watching a toddler attempt to operate a complex apparatus, "Do you know nothing of me? I am the best assassin the [[Backrooms Investigation Foundation|B.I.F.]] has ever known! Such feeble attempts at murder ca—" He stopped short, clutching his chest in agony, before beginning to slowly sink into the ground as if he were melting. With his last breath, he hoarsely whispered, "Curse you, '''Agent Iota'''! Curse your entire bloodline!" Smiling contentedly at the dissolving form of '''Agent Theta''', '''Sam Blue''' blew out his smoking gun. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''''Level 799''' no longer exists.''
